title: Schedule 1: Agri-Food Pilot (IMM 0114) - Canada.ca
content:
## Schedule 1: Agri-Food Pilot (IMM 0114)
Schedule 1: Agri-Food Pilot [IMM 0114] (PDF, 2.2 MB)

### How to download, save and open this form
- Use your computer. The form may not open on tablets or mobile phones .
- Save the form on your computer in a place you can remember. For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form. If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form.
- If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- After you download the form, open it using Adobe Acrobat Reader.

#### Open this form in Acrobat Reader
- Open Acrobat Reader. You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms. Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms.
- Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- Select “ File ” from the top menu.
- Find the location where you saved the form, click on the file, and click “ Open. ”
Read the step by step instructions on how to complete the form.

### Agri-Food Pilot – Schedule 1 [IMM 0114]

#### Section 1: Principal Applicant Information
- Enter your family name
- Enter your given name
- Enter your date of birth
- Enter your Unique Client Identifier
- Enter your passport number
- Enter your passport date of issue
- Enter your passport date of expiration
- Enter your country or territory of birth
- Enter your country of citizenship
- Enter your country of residence
- Enter your marital status
- Enter the number of expected accompanying family members
- Enter your telephone number/extension
- Enter the street, number, city, province/territory (if applicable), country and
                    postal code of
                    residential address
- Enter the street, number, city, province/territory (if applicable), country and
                    postal code of mailing
                    address (if not the same as residential address)

#### Section 2: Offer of employment (if applicable)
Note: If you reside in Canada and choose to meet the education requirements,
                you are not
                required to complete this section.
Refer to the Offer of Employment for Foreign National – Agri-Food Pilot form (IMM 0115) (opens in a new tab) provided by your employer to complete this section.
Enter the name of the business indicated in your offer of employment form
Enter the Canada Revenue Agency number included in the job offer.
Enter the address of the location where you will be physically working (street, number,
                  city and postal
                  code).
Enter the province/territory where you will be physically working.
Enter the employer’s contact email address (if applicable).
Enter the employer’s business website address (if applicable).
Enter the job title of the position offered by the employer.
Enter the National Occupation
                  Classification (NOC) code
                  of the position offered to you by the employer.
Enter the North
                  American Industry
                  Classification System (NAICS) code for the industry that the employer is engaged
                  in.
Enter the wage offered by the employer.
The wage offered to the employee must be one of the following:
- at or above the prevailing wage for the occupation in the province where the
                    employment will take place
                    (as identified by the Government of Canada’s Job Bank ),
                    unless a provincial wage
                    is not available, then the national prevailing wage applies
- determined for unionized workers as per the provisions of the collective agreement
                    covering the
                    occupation
The prevailing wage is the median wage of the occupation, as shown on the Job Bank website,
                  for the province
                  where the work is performed. If there is no prevailing wage shown for the province,
                  then the national
                  prevailing wage applies.
Enter the total number of work hours offered by the employer
Does the job offer meet all the following requirements?
- Full-time position (at least 30 hours/week)
- Indeterminate (no end date provided)
- The work is for employment outside of Quebec
- Non-seasonal, all year round work
The job offer must meet all of the requirements above in order to be valid.

#### Section 3: Work Experience
In this section, list the positions you have held in Canada within the last three years starting
              with the most
              recent.
Enter the business name of the employer
Enter the start date and end date of your employment. If you are currently working
                  for the same employer,
                  enter “Ongoing.”
Enter the street, number, city, province/territory, country and postal code of the work
                  location.
Enter the job title of the position.
Enter the National Occupational Classification (NOC) code of the position.
Enter the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for the primary
                  business activities your
                  employer is engaged in.
You can get this information from your employer reference letter, or the union reference
                  letter, for your
                  declared work experiences.
Enter the Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA) number of the work permit you held for
                  this position. If you
                  are using experience gained under an open work permit for vulnerable workers, enter the application
                  number of
                  the open work permit you held while working in this position.
Enter a contact name for employment reference. This should be an immediate supervisor or
                  personnel officer
                  working at the business, or your union representative.
Enter the telephone number of your employment reference(s) or union representative.
Enter the email address of your employment reference(s) or union representative.

#### Section 4: Language Proficiency Results
Enter the name of the approved language testing agency. See valid language
                  test results for more information.
Enter the date when you received your language test results. You can find this
                  information on your language
                  test results page. This could also be the date the testing official signed them.
Enter the date when you took the test. You can find this information on your language
                  test results page.
Enter your scores in the appropriate fields for speaking, reading, listening and writing.

#### Section 5: Education (if applicable)
Note: If you reside in Canada and choose to meet the job offer requirements,
                you are not
                required to complete this section.
From the list, select your highest level of education. See education
                  requirements for more information.
The level of education selected in this section should correspond to the level of
                  education completed in
                  Canada. If you earned your credential abroad, to the level shown on the Educational
                  Credential Assessment.
Enter the date you successfully completed your studies (graduation date).
Enter the name of the educational institution where you completed your studies.
Enter the street, number, city/town, province/territory/state, country and postal code of
                  the educational
                  institution.
If you have a foreign credential, enter the information found on your Educational
                  Credential Assessment
                  (ECA). See How
                  to get an educational credential assessment (ECA) for a list of IRCC designated
                  organizations.
Enter the name of the organization that issued your ECA, date your ECA was issued (year,
                  month and day) and
                  your ECA certificate number.

#### Section 6: Settlement Funds
Check the boxes to confirm that you understand the funds requirement and attest that you have
              sufficient funds to
              meet the requirements, if necessary.
Enter the amount of transferable and available funds you have unencumbered by debts or
              obligations, in Canadian
              dollars.

#### Section 7: Temporary Residence Status
If you’re currently residing in Canada, select your status and the expiry date. If you are on an
              implied
              status, then indicate the expiry date of your most recent temporary residence status.

#### Section 8: Declaration of application
- Signature of principal applicant
- Date (year, month, day)

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
